high_criticality_interfaces_top_priority.why_priority: Hard floor EC10=100%. Any unresolved claim blocks spec emission. Highest blast radius for founder UX.
Owns the EC9↔EC11 roof tradeoff (-2, strongest). Stale-cache hazard is a correctness concern.
Customer-system boundary. Overhead violation = contractual breach. M5 pinned MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT=2% as non-negotiable.
Enforces reject-on-violation. If backpressure fails, SS9 can breach overhead silently.
Rank-1 imputed importance (EC8=1.00). All agent work depends on this call succeeding. Provider-fallback chain depth only 2.
Streaming chunk cadence hazard (EC16). Partial completions must be invalid.
Mode-lock invariant violation = wrong provider fan-out across 5 parallel agents.
Gated on citation check; cascades to CLI emission. A false-positive pass here is a correctness disaster.
Non-invasiveness hard constraint (UC11). Write-scope leak = security + trust violation.

roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.m5_constraint: Probe SDK ↔ Collector contract must expose joint tuning
roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.m6_resolution: IF-17/IF-18 jointly carry probe_frequency + aggregation_window as one config push; backpressure triggers reject-on-violation.
roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.residual_risk_for_fmea: If collector crashes mid-window, SDK may not receive updated cfg and silently drift. FMEA should cover.

roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.m5_constraint: Batch size and aggregation window must not starve each other
roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.m6_resolution: IF-17 time-based flush fallback: after 60-min window, flush whatever batch ≥10 (flush_fallback_min).
roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.residual_risk_for_fmea: Flush_fallback_min=10 is an Estimate. If too small, collector gets tiny batches; if too large, starvation persists. FMEA should probe sensitivity.

roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.m5_constraint: Routing policy decision must precede parallel dispatch
roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.m6_resolution: IF-09 synchronous write + IF-03 mode-lock invariant (see SEQ-3).
roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.residual_risk_for_fmea: If IF-09 ack is lost and IF-03 fires anyway (race condition), up-to-5 agents fan out to wrong provider. FMEA must specify detection + recovery.

roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.m5_constraint: Traceback cache TTL and vendor-doc refresh cadence must invalidate together
roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.m6_resolution: SS7 internal invalidation protocol (SEQ-2): fetcher refresh triggers INVALIDATE all entries keyed to source_uri.
roof_tradeoffs_resolved_in_m6.residual_risk_for_fmea: Invalidation event lost between fetcher and cache = stale citations served. FMEA must model this path (likely highest severity given -2 roof weight).

error_contracts_requiring_fmea.blast_radius: customer contract breach + overhead SLA violation
CLI bundle delivered but unauditable; silent success/failure

failure_modes: race: dispatch fires before IF-09 ack
error_contracts_requiring_fmea.blast_radius: wrong provider used; 5 parallel agents all wrong

failure_modes: vault returns write-scope grant by mistake
JSONL schema mismatch on retention migration
error_contracts_requiring_fmea.blast_radius: compliance evidence gap; EC18 retention SLA violation

system_flows_handoff: Founder Intake → Spec → CLI Emission (5-step Quick Start)
open_questions_for_module_7: What MTTD and recovery action are acceptable for a stale SS7 cache entry that outlived a refresh? (drives FMEA severity + detection budget for the -2 roof pair)
Can IF-24 audit remain 'best-effort' or does EC18 compliance require synchronous-blocking audit writes? (informs SS3 pipeline blocking behavior)
How is a mid-session mode switch (cloud→local) handled — lock-break, new session, or error? (SEQ-3 implicitly assumes boot-only mode lock)
Is SS4 provider fallback depth = 2 sufficient for PC.4 (spec-gen time) under degraded-provider conditions? (informs final value of SS4_PROVIDER_FALLBACK_DEPTH)
Under what failure mode can SS9 silently exceed MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT = 2% despite IF-18 backpressure? (contractual + trust-critical scenario)
If SS10 buffer overflows before SS11 write succeeds, what is the acceptable data-loss window? (IF-19 doesn't specify; M5 AGGREGATION_WINDOW_MIN=60 implies up to 1 hr is tolerable but not approved)
What is the FMEA scope for dual-mode: does Privacy Local inherit the same failure modes as SOTA Cloud or a reduced subset (no provider fallback)?
